description_long: This lamp features a cylindrical, metallic base with a dark, possibly bronze or blackened finish, giving it a sturdy and industrial appearance. Rising from the base is a slender, wire-like stem that branches out into a fan-shaped wire frame, which likely serves as a decorative or protective element for the light source. The wire frame is composed of several evenly spaced, curved segments that create a petal or shell-like pattern, adding a touch of elegance to the utilitarian base. The lamp does not appear to have a traditional lampshade, instead showcasing its exposed wire structure as a design highlight. The overall form is minimalistic yet visually striking, suitable for modern urban environments such as lofts, cafes, or public spaces. The materials and construction suggest durability and a focus on both function and artistic flair.
description: A modern lamp with a dark cylindrical base and an elegant, fan-shaped wire frame extending upward, combining industrial and decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full fan-shaped wire frame and the cylindrical base directly below it.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the wire frame and the cylindrical base, with the wire segments appearing more compressed.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the symmetrical wire frame and the back of the cylindrical base, mirroring the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the curvature of the wire frame and the rounded base, similar to view 1 but from the other side.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
